title: Grade 9
description: Algebra, literature, physical science. Introduction to high school.
keywords: ninth grade, freshman, algebra 1, functions, polynomials, equations, statistics, histograms, scatter plots, 9th
documentation:
Core Subjects & Standards

Math

Math: Algebra I - Module 1

Module 1 focuses on Relationships Between Quantities and Reasoning with Equations and Their Graphs. This module sets the foundation for high school algebra.

Topic A: Introduction to Functions

Students explore graphing stories, including piecewise linear functions, quadratic functions, and exponential functions. They learn to analyze graphs representing real world scenarios like elevation versus time and water usage.

N-Q.A.1, N-Q.A.2, N-Q.A.3, A-CED.A.2
Topic B: The Structure of Expressions

Focuses on algebraic expressions, applying the distributive, commutative, and associative properties. Students master adding, subtracting, and multiplying polynomials.

A-SSE.A.2, A-APR.A.1
Topic C & Beyond: Equations and Inequalities

Students learn to solve complex equations, inequalities, and systems of equations, applying them to real world word problems like calculating ticket sale combinations and income tax rates.

A-CED.A.3, A-REI.C.6

Mathematics: Algebra I - Module 2

Module 2 dives into Descriptive Statistics, helping students summarize, represent, and interpret data on a single count or measurement variable as well as data on two categorical and quantitative variables.

Topics A & B: Shapes, Centers, and Variability

Students explore data distributions using dot plots and histograms. They calculate the center and spread of data using mean, median, standard deviation, and interquartile range to compare different data sets.

S-ID.A.1, S-ID.A.2, S-ID.A.3
Topic C: Categorical Data on Two Variables

Focuses on summarizing bivariate categorical data using two way frequency tables and calculating conditional relative frequencies to find associations.

S-ID.B.5, S-ID.C.9
Topic D: Numerical Data on Two Variables

Students create scatter plots, fit linear models to data, and analyze residual plots to evaluate how well a model fits the data. They also explore correlation and causation.

S-ID.B.6, S-ID.C.7

Mathematics: Algebra I - Module 3: Linear and Exponential Functions

Linear and Exponential Sequences

Understanding integer sequences, recursive and explicit formulas, and the difference between arithmetic and geometric patterns.

F-IF.A.3, F-BF.A.1a, F-LE.A.2
Exponential Growth & Decay

Modeling real-world scenarios like population growth and compound interest using exponential functions.

F-LE.A.1, F-LE.A.3
Functions and Their Graphs

Evaluating functions, interpreting domain and range, and analyzing transformations of graphs including piecewise and step functions.

F-IF.A.1, F-IF.B.4, F-IF.C.7

Mathematics: Algebra I - Module 4: Polynomial and Quadratic Expressions, Equations, and Functions

Multiplying and Factoring Polynomials

Mastering greatest common factor (GCF), trinomial factoring, and advanced strategies for quadratic expressions.

A-SSE.A.2, A-APR.A.1
Solving Quadratic Equations

Using the zero product property, completing the square, and the quadratic formula to find roots in real world contexts.

A-REI.B.4b, A-CED.A.1
Graphing and Modeling Quadratics

Analyzing key features of parabolas such as symmetry, vertices, and intercepts, while modeling projectile motion like free fall.

F-IF.C.7a, F-IF.B.4

Mathematics: Algebra I - Module 5: A Synthesis of Modeling with Equations and Functions

Elements of Modeling

Analyzing graphs, data sets, and verbal descriptions to identify key features and select appropriate linear, quadratic, or exponential models.

N-Q.A.2, F-LE.A.1
The Modeling Cycle

Moving from context to equations by identifying variables, formulating models, and interpreting solutions within real-world constraints.

A-CED.A.2, F-BF.A.1a
Comparing Function Types

Evaluating the growth rates and behaviors of different functions, such as comparing exponential growth to linear or quadratic models over time.

F-IF.B.6, F-LE.A.3

Note: The mathematics curriculum is sourced from EngageNY.

ELA

Literary theory, comprehensive research papers, classical literature.
